architecture.summary: A three-stage retrieval-augmented LLM pipeline: (1) a trainable small language model rewrites the input query to better match needed knowledge, (2) a web search engine retrieves contexts using the rewritten query, (3) a frozen black-box LLM reader generates answers from the retrieved contexts. The rewriter is trained via reinforcement learning using feedback signals from the frozen reader.

biblio.title: Query Rewriting in Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models

claims.statement: A rewrite-retrieve-read framework is proposed that pays attention to adaptation of the search query itself, addressing the gap between input text and needed knowledge in retrieval
A trainable scheme is proposed where a small language model acts as a rewriter trained using feedback from the LLM reader by reinforcement learning to better align the query to the frozen reader
Experiments show consistent performance improvement on downstream tasks including open-domain QA and multiple-choice QA
The framework is proven effective and scalable, bringing a new framework for retrieval-augmented LLM
